patient_information.diagnosis: Status post right anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) reconstruction with hamstring autograft; associated right knee pain, weakness, and decreased range of motion
patient_information.precautions: Weight bearing as tolerated with hinged knee brace locked in extension for ambulation until cleared by surgeon. Avoid pivoting, twisting, running, jumping, or cutting movements. No open-chain resisted knee extension 0–30 degrees for first 6 weeks. Monitor for signs of DVT or infection (increased swelling, redness, warmth, fever).

treatment_plan.post_operative_rehabilitation_protocol_f: Right knee ACL reconstruction with hamstring autograft (non-meniscal repair protocol)
treatment_plan.other_first_line: Emphasis on gradual return-to-running progression beginning around weeks 10–12 if strength, ROM, and functional testing meet protocol criteria; coordinate with surgeon before initiating higher-level plyometrics or sport-specific drills.
treatment_plan.other_second_line: Focus on right quadriceps activation (e.g., NMES as needed), hip and core strengthening, and progressive closed-chain functional training. Please provide periodic written or electronic progress updates at 4, 8, and 12 weeks to the referring surgeon’s office for return-to-sport planning.

special_instructions.special_instructions_first_line: Patient is a 29-year-old recreational soccer player and software engineer who is highly motivated to return to cutting and pivoting sports but understands need for a structured, criterion-based progression. Prior history of mild patellofemoral pain in the same knee—monitor patellar tracking and anterior knee pain with strengthening progression.
special_instructions.special_instructions_second_line: No known drug allergies. Non-smoker, generally healthy. Lives in a two-story townhouse and must navigate stairs daily; please include stair training and functional strategies for safe descent/ascent as strength improves. Encourage adherence to home exercise program 5–6 days per week and educate on appropriate use of ice and activity modification after longer workdays at his desk.
